sound_type: guttural bark -- low, resonant, short alarm bark. Soft grunts, moans, growls during social contact. Underwater clicks and roars during breeding. E=4
ecosystem: Haul-out rocks, sandy beaches, tidal flats, estuaries, harbors. Resident year-round in Puget Sound. Most common marine mammal in PNW.
season: Year-round resident. Does not migrate. Pupping: late spring to early summer. Present in Puget Sound every month.

coordinates.description: Harbor Seal in Puget Sound / San Juan Islands. Haul-out rocks throughout the Sound. Year-round resident.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST MARINE MAMMAL in the engine. FIRST PHOCIDAE (true seals). Class Mammalia returns (last seen ~degree 108). Massive taxonomic event at HALFWAY MARK (180/360). The engine enters the ocean.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Phoca from Greek 'phoke' (seal). vitulina from Latin 'vitulus' (calf) -- calf-like. 'Harbor Seal' for its preference for sheltered harbors and bays.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Phoca -- harbor seals and spotted seals. 2 species. The most widespread Phocid genus.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. FIRST Carnivora in SPS. Pinnipedia (seals, sea lions, walruses) is a suborder/clade within Carnivora.
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Phocidae. First marine mammal. First Carnivora. Taxonomic revolution at the midpoint.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia returns to the SPS engine. The engine expands from primarily avian to multi-class at degree 180.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual bark: 0.2-0.5 seconds. Series: 1-10 barks. Social sounds continuous at haul-outs.
song_metrics.note_structure: Low, guttural bark. Short, sharp. Also: soft grunts, moans, growls. Underwater: clicks, roars, low-frequency sounds.
song_metrics.note_types: Alarm bark (primary above-water), social grunts/moans/growls, mother-pup recognition calls, underwater breeding vocalizations.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Bark series: 1-10 barks in sequence during alarm. Social sounds: continuous murmur at haul-outs.
song_metrics.timbre: Guttural, resonant, low. Bark is sharp and purposeful. Social sounds are softer, more varied.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Moderate volume. Carries across water. Purposeful rather than overwhelming. Intermittent.
song_metrics.tempo: Bark series: ~1-3 per second during alarm. Social sounds: irregular, continuous.
song_metrics.context: Haul-out sites. Alarm, territorial, social, mother-pup. Also underwater during breeding.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Bark consistent. Individual recognition by mother-pup calls.
song_metrics.key_property: GUTTURAL BARK + LIQUID GAZE. FIRST MARINE MAMMAL. FIRST PHOCIDAE. Haul-out rocks. Resident. HALFWAY MARK. E=4. v1.49.320.

pelage.overall: Variable: silver-gray to dark brown with spots and rings. Pattern unique to individual (like fingerprints). Short, dense fur. Streamlined body.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Spotted/ringed pattern. Round face. Large dark eyes (liquid gaze). No external ear flaps (vs sea lions). Short flippers.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Annual molt replaces fur. Pattern remains individually unique.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Powerful undulating hind-flipper propulsion. Streamlined. Can reach speeds of 19 km/h. Dives to 500+ meters.

physical_description.overall: Length: 1.5-1.8 m. Weight: 45-130 kg. Round face. Large dark eyes. Thick vibrissae. No external ears.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Large dark eyes (liquid gaze). Round face. Spotted pelage. No external ear flaps. Short fore-flippers.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-sized seal on rocks or swimming. Round face with large dark eyes. Spotted/ringed pattern. No external ears (vs sea lions). Listen for guttural bark when alarmed.

nodes.title: First Marine Mammal -- Taxonomic Revolution
nodes.description: FIRST MARINE MAMMAL in the engine. FIRST PHOCIDAE. Class Mammalia returns. The engine enters the ocean at the midpoint (180/360). Taxonomic scope expands dramatically.

nodes.title: The Threshold -- Between Land and Sea
nodes.description: Haul-out behavior = living at the boundary between worlds. On rocks: terrestrial, breathing air. In water: marine predator, diving 500m. Two worlds. The midpoint embodied.

nodes.title: The Liquid Gaze -- Large Dark Eyes
nodes.description: Disproportionately large, dark eyes adapted for underwater vision. Round face. Vibrissae (whiskers) detect fish by water turbulence. The most compelling marine encounter in PNW.

nodes.title: Halfway Mark -- 180 / 360 = 50.0%
nodes.description: The engine's exact midpoint. 180 species documented. The first half is complete. The second half begins. At the midpoint, the engine enters a new taxonomic realm.

cross_references.relationship: TAXONOMIC SHIFT! After 6 consecutive Alcidae (174-179, birds), the engine enters marine mammals at 180. Bird-to-mammal transition at the midpoint.
MARINE PREDATORS! Murre: dives 100m for fish. Seal: dives 500m for fish. Both marine predators. Different classes (Aves vs Mammalia). Same ocean.
CONSERVATION! Murrelet: ESA Threatened. Seal: MMPA protected. Both at the intersection of conservation law and marine ecology.
HALFWAY MARK PAIR! Both at 180. Both at the threshold. Macklemore between eras. Harbor Seal between land and sea. The threshold.
